text: The surgeon uses an Alexis wound retractor to safely reduce the exteriorized bowel through a small midline laparotomy incision, which extends from the umbilical port site. The retractor provides protection and retraction for the wound following colonic mobilization and ileocolic pedicle ligation.

The surgeon returns the resected bowel to the abdomen by unfolding the white ring of the Alexis wound retractor, allowing the bowel to fill the Alexis tunnel. A twist in the Alexis provides a seal, and gentle pressure is applied to slide the bowel smoothly through the tunnel. The Alexis is untwisted, and the green ring is removed from the abdomen, allowing for normal wound closure.
